window_summary: This window tracks the recent mui/material-ui release stream from v5.18.0 through v9.0.1, including major, minor, and patch releases, as well as alpha and beta pre-releases. It covers new features like Tailwind CSS v4 support, nativeColor, modular CSS layers, and the stable v9.0 release with accessibility improvements and deprecated API cleanup.

chunks.memory_unit_text: In the mui/material-ui release v7.0.2, the Autocomplete component gains the ability to render a custom single value and prevents shrink animation in controlled mode when an initial value is provided. The AvatarGroup fixes the spacing prop to accept value 0. The Dialog component deprecates composed classes. The material-nextjs package fixes nonce issues and adds warnings to the Next.js router integration. The styled-engine adds caching to StyledEngineProvider to improve Jest test performance. Various core, docs, and infrastructure improvements are included.

chunks.memory_unit_text: In the mui/material-ui release v7.1.0, Material UI now works with Tailwind CSS v4. The InputBase fixes a text cursor jump when inserting text fragments with new lines. The OutlinedInput adds a missing notchedOutline slot. The Snackbar skips default onClickAway when defaultMuiPrevented is provided. The Avatar fixes img slot types and adds missing slots. The Badge, BottomNavigationAction, and CardActionArea components add slots and slotProps. The useMediaQuery hook adds a warning for print usage. The material-nextjs package adds an option to enable CSS layers for pages router and fixes @layer order rules in App Router. The system package fixes types organization. The styled-engine infers ownerState from props in styled and fixes style overrides variants type. Docs include various updates and a Tailwind CSS v4 integration guide.

chunks.memory_unit_text: In the mui/material-ui release v7.2.0, a modularCssLayers theme option is added for splitting styles into multiple CSS layers. An example for using Material UI with React Router v7 is provided. The Backdrop fixes handling of the component prop. The Chip explicitly defines line-height and fixes event handler handling. The OutlinedInput and Input deprecate composed classes. The Select passes MenuProps.slotProps.list alongside MenuListProps. Romanian locale translation is enabled for labelDisplayedRows. The system package adds the modularCssLayers theme flag. The styled-engine avoids reusing the emotion cache across SSR requests. The codemod adds a Dialog codemod for deprecated props. Docs include various updates and a new example.

Tag: v7.3.0; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2025-08-05T05:41:42Z; created_at: 2025-08-05T05:40:12Z
Release notes:
A big thanks to the 24 contributors who made this release possible. Here are some highlights ✨:
- 🚀 Shipped `nativeColor` feature that eliminates JS color manipulation and unlocks support for all modern color spaces (#43942) @siriwatknp!
To learn more, check out the [Native Color](https://mui.com/material-ui/customization/css-theme-variables/native-color/) guide.
### `@mui/material@7.3.0`
- Support native color without JS manipulation (#43942) @siriwatknp

- [Switch] Use `slotProps.input` instead of deprecated `inputProps` for accessible controlled switch (#46625) @adiitxa
### Core

chunks.memory_unit_text: In the mui/material-ui release v7.3.0, the nativeColor feature is shipped, eliminating JS color manipulation and unlocking support for all modern color spaces. The Accordion adds a region slot. The SpeedDial fixes arrow key navigation when slotProps.fab is defined. The Select improves MenuProps.slotProps implementation and implements pointer cancellation. The Switch adds role="switch". The Autocomplete fixes auto highlight when options change but not length. The codemod fixes the v5.0.0/top-level-imports codemod changing color imports. Docs include various updates and a new dashboard with CRUD template.

chunks.memory_unit_text: In the mui/material-ui release v7.3.6, the Accordion moves properties to AccordionOwnProps. The Autocomplete removes an unnecessary dependency, fixes input caret not showing after chip navigation, and fixes an ArrowLeft crash. The Button fixes running formAction when passed. The Chip removes a leftover closing parenthesis in CSS class key. The ListItem adds a secondaryAction slot. The NumberField fixes scroll behavior. The Select fixes keyboard navigation in shadow DOM and passing certain event handlers. The Slider accepts readonly array for marks. The Snackbar avoids unnecessary ownerState spread. The TextField allows custom props in slot props via TS module augmentation. The Tabs fixes Arrow key navigation in shadow DOM. Theme typings for applyStyles with custom color schemes are fixed. The system package fixes an unwanted attribute on DOM from InitColorSchemeScript. The lab package fixes Masonry layout flicker and single column issue. Docs include various updates and a new Number Field component page.

chunks.memory_unit_text: In the mui/material-ui release v7.3.7, the Accordion removes unnecessary handling of the square prop. The Alert removes unnecessary default icon mapping fallback. The AppBar fixes inherit color CSS variable not getting applied. The Autocomplete fixes ArrowLeft, Backspace, and Delete behavior for multiple and single-value rendering with proper caret handling. The Backdrop removes unnecessary passing of classes from root slot. The ButtonGroup fixes styles when variant is contained. The CardActionArea removes incorrect root ref forwarding. The Checkbox fixes readonly checkboxes. The ClickAwayListener tightens the parameter type of createHandleSynthetic. The Dialog fixes backdrop theme style overrides. The Select fixes dropdown width not matching trigger width on window resize. The Tabs fixes passing incorrect slot name props. Docs include various updates and enable MUI chat on demos.

The IconButton removes unused color palette mapping. Icons revert to using wildcard export paths. The Input fixes layout shift with display: flex and prevents the notched prop from leaking into the DOM. The InputBase places aria-label on the input element. Inputs fix autofocus in SSR environment. The List fixes sticky subheader overlapping iOS scrollbar. The Popper persists positioning styles when popperOptions changes reference. The Select stops using combobox element for labeling and supports single-pointer-cycle selection and pointer cancellation. The Switch fixes incorrect role with slotProps.input. The Tabs fix scrollable tabs blocking pointer events. The TextField fixes autofill styles always in dark mode when CSS variables are used. The Tooltip simplifies RTL styles using CSS logical properties and closes when its child becomes disabled. The Typography fixes the h4 variant when using responsiveFontSizes(). The useMediaQuery fixes a crash in Firefox extension. The system package fixes theme mutation when using responsive typography shorthand in sx. The codemod adds a --jsx option and packageName support. The utils add shadow dom utils, fix resolveProps receiving empty string className, and remove unnecessary excludeKeys from extractEventHandlers. questions.query_text: In the mui/material-ui releases, which release introduces the nativeColor feature that eliminates JS color manipulation and unlocks support for all modern color spaces?

answer_support.answer_text: the nativeColor feature is shipped, eliminating JS color manipulation and unlocking support for all modern color spaces

questions.query_text: Across the mui/material-ui releases, how does the CSS layers feature differ between v5.18.0 and v7.2.0?

options.text: v5.18.0 backports CSS layers from v7, while v7.2.0 introduces a modularCssLayers theme option for splitting styles into multiple CSS layers.
v5.18.0 introduces CSS layers as a new feature, while v7.2.0 removes CSS layers support.
Both releases only update the styled-engine caching without any CSS layers changes.
v5.18.0 adds CSS layers to the system package only, while v7.2.0 adds it to the material package only.

questions.expected_answer: v5.18.0 backports CSS layers from v7, while v7.2.0 introduces a modularCssLayers theme option for splitting styles into multiple CSS layers.

a modularCssLayers theme option is added for splitting styles into multiple CSS layers
